title: practical english usage - Part 328
content:
We   use in and on after sit down and arrive.

Throw another log on the fire.

- He was trying to throw his hat onto the roof Could you put the ham in the fridge?

- In the experiment, we put glowing magnesium into jars of oxygen.

vocabulary •  500  instead and instead of not I'll have tea instead of coffee, please, (     ... instead coffee...) Instead is not used alone as a preposition; we  use the two words instead of.

500 instead        and   instead        of ‘ Henry made a fool of himself' ‘ He did indeed?

‘ It's cold.' ‘ It is indeed.' We are indeed interested in your offer, and would be glad to have prices.

short answers (►  308).

confirmation or emphatic agreement. This is rather formal. It is common in Indeed can also be used after be or an auxiliary verb in order to suggest 2  indeed        with    verb not He-was-driving-quite/extremely fast indeed.

xor H^tvas-drMng-fdsHHtieetk extremely or quite.

Indeed is unusual in this sense without very, and is not normally used after He was driving very fast indeed.  Thank you very much  indeed.

I was very pleased indeed to hear from you.

formal, and is unusual in American English.

Indeed can be used to emphasise very with an adjective or adverb. This is rather 1  very    ...  indeed 499 indeed ►    465 in   the    end,    at   the    end,    at   last    and   finally In more formal English, despite can be used in the same way as in spite of This is rather heavy: although means the same, and is more common.

He is good company, in spite of the fact that he talks all the time.

in spite of the fact that.

In spite of cannot be followed directly by a t/ɪɑf-elause. Instead, we  can use In spite of having a headache I enjoyed the film.

In spite of can be followed by an -ing form.

She passed her exams because of her wonderful teacher.

She passed her exams in spite of her terrible teacher.

In spite of is the opposite of because of. Compare: accent.) We understood him  in spite of his accent. (= ... although he had a strong We went out in spite of the rain. (= ... although it was raining.) the same as although + clause.

In spite of is used as a preposition. In spite of + noun means more or less 498 in   spite      of  _____________________ Word  Problems from A  to Z      Section 31 vocabulary *501  its and it's It's time for her to go to bed.

To  say who  should do something, we  use for + object + infinitive (►   113).

It's time to buy a new car.

It's time (or it is time) can be followed by an infinitive.

1  followed       by   infinitive 502 it's   time There is a similar difference between whose and who's, ►    629.   For more about contractions, ►    337.

Have you seen my camera? It's disappeared. (     ... Its disappeared.) not It's raining again. (     Its raining again.) It's is the contracted form of it is or it has.

Every country has its traditions. (     .. it's traditions.) Its is a possessive word (like my, your).

foreign learners.

These two words are often confused by native speakers of English as well as by 501 its   and   it's not (    Instead, he's very generous.) He's a rather anti-social person. On  the other hand, he's very generous.

We do not use instead to mark a contrast, in the sense of 'on the other hand’ .

Don't marry Jake. Marry me instead.

She didn't go to Greece after all. Instead, she went to America.

Instead (without of) is an adverb. It usually begins or ends a clause.

3 adverb:     instead telling her mother.) not her mother should go together.) (    She often goes swimming instead of She often goes swimming without telling her mother. (Swimming and telling not school.) (     She often goes swimming without going to school.) - She often goes swimming instead of going to school. (Swimming replaces (Normally they would have gone together.) Max and Jake were invited, but Max was ill, so Jake went without him.

(Lou replaced Ruth.) (     ... Lou went without her.) - Ruth was invited to the reception, but she was ill, so Lou went instead of her.

suggests that one person, thing, etc is not together with another. Compare: Instead of suggests that one person, thing or action replaces another.  Without 2  instead        of   and   without go to work.) not I stayed in bed all day instead of going to work. (     .. .instead of (to) Instead of is not usually followed by an infinitive.
